calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Called with Line 1. 592890, Voung vs. Harvey.

Appearances via Zoom:
Alexander Weyland, Esq., 415-536-2800, appearing for Defendant
Appearances in person:
Vladdie Viltman, Esq., 415-673-5600, appearing for Plaintiff.

Argued. Mr. Viltman stated that he contested the tentative, requested clarification on the tentative ruling and provided oral argument to support his position. Mr. Weyland argued that the ruling to consolidate has been entered and that the points brought up have already been decided by the Court.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is DENIED. The commonality of law and fact involved in this dispute may lead to inconsistent rulings. CCP 1281.2(c).
Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Vedica Puri, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D210)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-03-2022 Continued To Jun-10-22 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 210
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is transferred to department 210 to be heard at 8:30 a.m. on June 10, 2022 per the order of the presiding judge. The Honorable Charles Haines recused himself.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
